paper_title: On cuts of small chromatic number in sparse graphs
summary: Conjecture 1.3 is disproved in the same paper that states it. The authors prove that the conjecture 'fails dramatically': whereas Conjecture 1.3 asserts ℓ_k = k for all k, the paper establishes that for every ε > 0 and all sufficiently large k, ℓ_k ≤ (1+ε)k/2, showing that the trivial lower bound ℓ_k ≥ k/2 is asymptotically tight. The paper is to appear in Discrete Mathematics. No subsequent follow-up papers addressing Conjecture 1.3 were found in the indexed literature.
search_queries: Aubian Bonamy Bourneuf Fontaine Picasarri-Arrieta "cuts" "chromatic number" "sparse graphs" 2510.01791 conjecture
notes: The conjecture is disproved within the source paper itself (arXiv:2510.01791). The paper proposes Conjecture 1.3 as the natural bold generalization of the confirmed cases ℓ_1=1, ℓ_2=2 and the conjectured ℓ_3=3, and then proves it is far from correct by showing ℓ_k ≤ (1+ε)k/2 asymptotically. No external follow-up papers were found; the disproof is complete and contained in the source paper.
